attributes.description: The campaign this membership record is part of.
attributes.name: campaign
attributes.usage: One of two foreign keys on the junction. Required.

attributes.description: The contact who is the campaign member.
attributes.name: contact
attributes.usage: Either contact or lead is set (not both, in Salesforce).

attributes.description: The lead who is the campaign member (when target is a Lead, not a Contact).
attributes.name: lead
attributes.usage: Salesforce uses LeadId on CampaignMember when the target hasn't been converted yet. Only one of LeadId or ContactId is populated per record.

attributes.description: Per-member status of participation in the campaign.
attributes.name: memberStatus
attributes.usage: Status values are configurable per-campaign — different status sets apply to different campaign types (email versus event versus direct mail). Salesforce's CampaignMemberStatus configuration table holds per-Campaign status options. Drives response-rate reporting.

attributes.description: Computed flag indicating whether this member has reached a "responded" status.
attributes.name: hasResponded
attributes.usage: Auto-derived from Status — Salesforce CampaignMemberStatus records have a HasResponded boolean that tags which statuses count as responses. Drives response-rate calculations.

attributes.description: Date the member first reached a "responded" status.
attributes.name: firstRespondedDate
attributes.usage: Drives time-to-response analysis (avg days from Sent to Responded per campaign type).

attributes.description: When the member was added to the campaign.
attributes.name: createdAt
attributes.usage: Anchors response-time calculations. Drives "added this week" reports during active campaigns.

business_context: Campaign Member records hold the per-recipient details of campaign participation. They answer: "Of the 10,000 people we sent this email to, how many opened it, clicked through, replied, registered for the webinar?" Each Campaign Member record represents one Contact-in-one- Campaign with one status. The status enum is the most-customized aspect — different campaign types need different statuses (Email campaigns track Sent / Opened / Clicked / Bounced; Event campaigns track Invited / Registered / Attended / No-Show; Direct Mail tracks Sent / Returned). Modeling differs across systems: Salesforce uses an explicit CampaignMember junction object with both ContactId and LeadId fields (since either can be the target). HubSpot uses Associations rather than a junction object — Contact-to-Campaign association carries status. EspoCRM uses Target Lists (linkMultiple) on Campaign rather than per-member junction records. CiviCRM uses ActivityContact + Activity-of-type-Campaign-Activity for similar effect. Bloomerang ties constituents to Campaigns via Transactions rather than membership. When interviewing clients, surface "Campaign Member" by asking whether they need to track per-recipient response status (not just aggregate response counts). If yes, target backend's Campaign Member modeling matters; if they only need aggregate metrics, this entity is optional.
name: Campaign Member
purpose: A junction record connecting a Contact (or Lead) to a Campaign, capturing per-recipient status: was sent the email, opened it, clicked through, registered for the event, attended, donated, declined.

relationships.description: A campaign member belongs to one campaign.
A campaign member typically references one contact.
A campaign member can alternatively reference a lead (in Salesforce/ EspoCRM) before conversion.

sources.title: CiviCRM: Email and Mass Mailings (campaign-member equivalent via activities)

systems.name: (no native concept)
systems.notes: Attio has no Campaign Member concept. Sequence enrollment and response tracking happens at the Sequence level rather than as a junction.

systems.name: (no Campaign Member concept; Transactions link to Campaign directly)
systems.notes: Bloomerang has no Campaign Member junction. Constituent-Campaign relationships are inferred through Transactions (a Transaction links to a Constituent and references a Campaign). Outreach without donation isn't tracked at the campaign-membership level.

systems.name: Activity (of type Campaign Activity)
systems.notes: CiviCRM uses Activities to record per-contact campaign actions (email sent, opened, responded). Activity records have activity_type_id pointing at CiviMail-style email activities and link to Campaign via campaign_id. Less of a true junction table.

systems.name: TargetList (linkMultiple on Campaign)
systems.notes: EspoCRM uses Target Lists rather than per-member junctions. A Target List is a collection of Contacts/Accounts/Leads/Users that can be attached to a Campaign. Per-member status is tracked on campaign-specific records (CampaignLogRecord) for sent/bounced tracking but lacks the rich status taxonomy of Salesforce CampaignMember.

systems.name: Contact-Campaign Association (no junction object)
systems.notes: HubSpot doesn't use a separate junction object. Contact-Campaign relationships are tracked via Associations + per-Contact event properties (last campaign, last email opened, last click, etc.). Less granular than Salesforce's per-member status tracking.

systems.name: CampaignMember
systems.notes: Standard junction object. Each CampaignMember has CampaignId, LeadId or ContactId (one or the other), Status (per-campaign- configurable), HasResponded (computed), FirstRespondedDate. Campaign Status values are configured per-Campaign via the "Advanced Setup" CampaignMemberStatus records.

systems.name: CampaignMember
systems.notes: Same as Salesforce. Heavily used in nonprofit deployments for appeal-response tracking — Sent / Donated / Declined / Renewed. Custom CampaignMemberStatus values typical per appeal type.
